pattern: Sort points by x-coordinate and greedily place each rectangle to cover as many rightward points as possible within width w.
hints: The y-dimension is always covered (rectangles extend from y=0 to infinity), so only the x-coordinates matter for counting rectangles.
Sort all unique x-values and use a greedy sweep: place a rectangle starting at the current leftmost uncovered x, which can reach x+w.
Advance past all points whose x falls within [current_x, current_x + w], then place the next rectangle at the next uncovered point.

edge_inputs.description: two points exactly w+1 apart — need 2 rectangles
multiple points at same x — only one rectangle needed for that column
w=0 so each unique x needs its own rectangle

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    pts = set()
    while len(pts) < size:
        x = rng.randint(0, 10000)
        y = rng.randint(0, 10000)
        pts.add((x, y))
    points = [list(p) for p in pts]
    w = rng.randint(0, 1000)
    return [points, w]
